The classification of Heleochloa pygmaea is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and then into the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Heleochloa pygmaea.

intro: Heleochloa pygmaea, also known as dwarf club sedge, is a small, tufted perennial plant native to New Zealand. The health benefits of Heleochloa pygmaea include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
